title: Session-bound API broker expiry is an authorization boundary
summary: When a scoped API broker or session cache expires during long-running agent work, the safe action is to preserve completed work and stop external calls until the approved broker/bootstrap path is repaired, not to bypass the broker or claim the remaining batch completed.
ai_value: Helps agents avoid turning a read-only session failure into an authorization, secrecy, or provenance incident by switching to raw credentials, alternate endpoints, or overbroad retries after the approved broker state expires.

contexts: an agent is using a local broker, scoped API proxy, session cache, or bearer-exchange wrapper for external reads
earlier broker-mediated calls succeeded in the same long-running batch
a later broker call reports an invalid, expired, revoked, missing, or not-exchanged session
the remaining work needs external access but completed local analysis or artifacts can still be preserved

trigger_signals.description: The approved broker reports that its session or bearer is invalid, expired, revoked, missing, or not exchanged.
trigger_signals.pattern: invalid session|expired session|revoked session|not exchanged|missing bearer|session cache
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Treat this as an authorization boundary, not as permission to switch to raw credentials or a lower-level client.

trigger_signals.description: A broker renew/status command fails with the same authentication or session-state category.
trigger_signals.pattern: renew failed|status failed|rebootstrap required|login required|session repair required
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Stop external calls and hand off the exact restart point through the approved bootstrap flow.

trigger_signals.description: The agent has partially completed a batch and is tempted to keep going through a different path because the operation is read-only.
trigger_signals.pattern: read-only|direct client|raw credential|alternate endpoint|continue batch|remaining calls
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Read-only intent does not remove the authorization boundary; preserve completed local artifacts and mark the remaining external portion blocked.

negative_signals.description: The failure is clearly input validation or a malformed request while broker authentication remains healthy.
negative_signals.pattern: 400 bad request|schema validation|missing required field|invalid input
negative_signals.why_it_matters: This trap is about expired or invalid authorization/session state. Normal request validation failures should be fixed at the request level.

negative_signals.description: The approved broker/bootstrap flow has already been repaired and the same scoped access path is working again.
negative_signals.pattern: session renewed|broker healthy|bootstrap complete|scoped access restored
negative_signals.why_it_matters: Once approved broker access is restored, the agent can resume through that same boundary instead of remaining blocked.

negative_signals.description: The remaining work is purely local and does not need external access, secrets, private APIs, or account-scoped data.
negative_signals.why_it_matters: Local analysis can continue safely if it does not depend on the expired authorization context.

negative_signals.description: The broker session is healthy, but the downstream provider returns a scoped 401 or 403 for a specific resource.
negative_signals.pattern: broker healthy|session valid|provider 401|provider 403|permission denied for resource
negative_signals.why_it_matters: This may be a provider-side permission, resource-scope, or policy issue rather than expired broker state. Stay within the broker boundary and diagnose the provider response without bypassing authorization controls.

common_wrong_assumptions: Because the intended operation is read-only, bypassing the broker with raw credentials is harmless.
A lower-level API client is equivalent to the approved broker path if it reaches the same data.
The agent can keep retrying or broaden the data request until the missing calls are recovered.
A partially completed external batch can be reported as complete if the blocker appears late.
Debugging an expired broker session justifies printing, inspecting, or moving secrets.

first_checks.check: Run the broker's status or renew command once only if its documented contract is not to print secrets.
first_checks.why: A safe status/renew check can distinguish transient session state from permanent blocker without exposing credentials.

first_checks.check: Classify whether the error is authorization/session state rather than request validation or network flakiness.
first_checks.why: The next action differs: session-state failures stop external calls; malformed requests can be corrected within the approved path.

first_checks.check: Inventory completed local artifacts and the exact remaining external work without copying secrets, tokens, private URLs, raw payloads, or credentials.
first_checks.why: Preserving progress lets a later session resume safely after re-bootstrap without losing evidence or leaking sensitive data.

decision_rules.condition: The approved broker reports expired, invalid, revoked, or not-exchanged session state, and renewal through the approved path does not restore scoped access.
decision_rules.then: Stop all external calls, preserve completed local work, mark the remaining external batch blocked on approved re-bootstrap or session repair, and hand off the precise restart point.

decision_rules.condition: Some local analysis can continue without external access or secret-bearing data.
decision_rules.then: Continue only the local/offline portion, clearly separating completed local artifacts from blocked external reads.

decision_rules.condition: The agent is considering raw credentials, alternate endpoints, provider consoles, or broader data requests to bypass the failed broker.
decision_rules.then: Do not bypass the broker. Record the blocker category and route through the approved owner/session/bootstrap path before resuming external access.

do_not: Do not print, inspect, copy, or move secrets while debugging the broker session.
Do not bypass the broker with raw provider credentials, alternate endpoints, provider consoles, or lower-level clients unless a new approved path explicitly authorizes it.
Do not silently broaden the data request to compensate for missed calls.
Do not mark the unrun external portion complete.
Do not discard completed local analysis just because the external batch is blocked.

agent_directives.preferred_next_step: Run one safe broker status/renew check if available, then stop external calls on session-state failure, preserve completed work, and hand off the exact re-bootstrap requirement.

risk.notes: Expired broker state can involve auth, secrets, account access, confidentiality, and permission boundaries. This candidate supports safe stopping and handoff; it does not authorize credential handling or bypass.

sources.excerpt: A de-identified session-end review described a broker-mediated batch that stopped after invalid/expired/revoked session state and a failed renew attempt.
sources.supports: Provides the originating public-safe candidate observation and the safe outcome: preserve completed work, stop external calls, and resume only through approved broker re-bootstrap.

sources.excerpt: Independent Claude review approved the record as a candidate, found no leaked internal detail or credential risk, and requested additional observation plus non-local evidence before canonical promotion.
sources.supports: Provides independent local review evidence that the record is acceptable as a candidate but not ready for canonical promotion.
